data.title: Phillips and Sing named PHFA 2026 housing policy fellows in Harrisburg. Funding to benefit policymakers and industry.
data.description: Two individuals, Zachary Phillips and Dr. Evaine K. Sing, have been selected as the recipients of the 2026 Kathy A. Possinger Housing Policy Fellowship by the Pennsylvania Housing Finance Agency. This funded research, announced in Harrisburg, Pennsylvania on December 11, 2025, aims to support policymakers and the affordable housing industry. This fellowship will provide valuable insights and recommendations that could potentially benefit policymakers and industry professionals in ensuring adequate and affordable housing solutions.

data.title: Planet Labs PBC beats Q3 sales estimates with $81.25 million revenue, raising 2026 guidance; lists breakeven adjusted earnings.
data.description: Planet Labs PBC, listed on NYSE as PL, exceeded expectations in its third-quarter sales with reported revenue of $81.25 million. This surpassed analyst predictions of $71.99 million. The company also adjusted its FY26 sales guidance, raising it above estimates. Despite reporting breakeven adjusted earnings per share, Planet Labs faced a loss of 19 cents per share on a GAAP basis. The company highlighted its strong performance in the third quarter, emphasizing accelerated revenue growth and progress towards profitability goals. Furthermore, Planet Labs showcased its success with AI-enabled global monitoring solutions, including recent recognitions such as an award under the NGA's Luno B program and expanded operations with NATO.

data.title: Adobe Inc (ADBE) beats Q4 earnings expectations with $5.50 EPS and $6.19 billion revenue. FY2026 EPS guidance at $17.90-18.10.
data.description: Adobe Inc. (NASDAQ: ADBE) exceeded expectations with their fourth-quarter earnings report. They reported earnings of $5.50 per share, surpassing the analyst estimate of $5.39. The quarterly revenue was $6.19 billion, beating the Street estimate of $6.11 billion. This shows growth from last year's revenue of $5.61 billion for the same period. Looking ahead, Adobe anticipates a fiscal 2026 GAAP EPS in the range of $17.90 to $18.10, higher than the analyst estimate of $16.56. The company attributes its success to the increasing significance in the global AI ecosystem and the rapid adoption of its AI-driven tools. For more details, the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Nextworld recognized as top LCAP and WMS platform for usability by Nucleus Research. DENVER, Dec. 11, 2025.
data.description: Nextworld, a cutting-edge AI platform, was ranked as the most user-friendly option for both low-code application platform (LCAP) and Warehouse Management System (WMS). The evaluation by Nucleus Research, unveiled in December 11, 2025, marked Nextworld's debut in their rankings.

data.title: Bhutan introduces sovereign-backed RWA token, mines Bitcoin, and adopts blockchain for financial system update.
data.description: A country in the Himalayas has started mining Bitcoin and has accumulated a significant amount of digital assets. They are also utilizing blockchain technology to update their financial infrastructure.

data.title: Global EBPP industry expected to reach 42.1 billion units by 2030, growing at a CAGR of 6.2% from 2024.
data.description: In Dublin, the "Electronic Bill Presentment and Payment (EBPP) - Global Strategic Business Report" is now available on ResearchAndMarkets.com. The worldwide Electronic Bill Presentment and Payment (EBPP) market reached 29.4 billion bills in 2024 and is expected to hit 42.1 billion bills by 2030. This represents a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 6.2% from 2024 to 2030. The report offers a detailed examination of market trends, drivers, and projections to assist in making informed decisions.

data.title: Klarna collaborates with Privy, a company owned by Stripe, to create a crypto wallet for the public.
data.description: A Swedish financial technology company, Klarna, is in the process of developing its personal cryptocurrency wallet in collaboration with Privy, a wallet infrastructure firm owned by Stripe.

data.title: Wall Street analysts have revised Lululemon's Q3 expectations, estimating earnings at $2.21/share, with revenue expected at $2.48 billion. Stock rose 2.9% to $187.62 ahead of earnings release on Dec. 11.
data.description: Athletic apparel company Lululemon Athletica Inc. (NASDAQ: LULU) is set to announce its third-quarter earnings after the market closes on Thursday, December 11. Analysts predict earnings of $2.21 per share for this quarter, a decrease from $2.87 per share in the same period last year. The estimated revenue for the quarter is $2.48 billion, compared to $2.4 billion in the previous year. In the second quarter, Lululemon reported revenue of $2.53 billion, slightly below the consensus estimate of $2.54 billion. Lululemon's stock price closed at $187.62, up 2.9% on Wednesday. For further information and recent analyst ratings, visit Benzinga's Analyst Stock Ratings page.

data.title: Adobe Inc. reported Q4 earnings of $5.50 per share, beating estimates of $5.39, with revenue of $6.19 billion compared to an estimate of $6.11 billion. They project fiscal 2026 GAAP EPS of $17.90 to $18.10.
data.description: Adobe Inc. (NASDAQ: ADBE) exceeded expectations with their fourth-quarter earnings report. The company reported earnings of $5.50 per share, surpassing the analyst estimate of $5.39 per share. Quarterly revenue stood at $6.19 billion, higher than the Street estimate of $6.11 billion, showing growth from the $5.61 billion revenue reported for the same period last year. Looking ahead, Adobe anticipates a fiscal 2026 GAAP EPS in the range of $17.90 to $18.10, above the analyst estimate of $16.56. The company attributed its strong performance to its increasing significance in the global AI ecosystem and the widespread adoption of its AI-powered tools. For more details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Synopsys beats Q4 earnings forecast, EPS at $2.90 vs $2.78 expected. Revenue at $2.26B vs $2.25B estimate. CEO optimistic. FY 2026 outlook strong.
data.description: Synopsys, Inc. (NASDAQ: SNPS) exceeded market expectations in its fourth-quarter earnings report. The company announced earnings of $2.90 per share, surpassing the projected $2.78. Quarterly revenue totaled $2.26 billion, slightly higher than the estimated $2.25 billion. Synopsys' CEO, Sassine Ghazi, expressed satisfaction with the results, highlighting the company's evolution into a leading provider of engineering solutions. Looking ahead, Synopsys anticipates adjusted earnings per share for fiscal 2026 to range between $14.32 and $14.40, surpassing the analyst prediction of $12.85. Additionally, the company expects fiscal revenue to fall within the range of $9.56 billion to $9.66 billion. For more details, the full story is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: PGP advises Dermatology Specialists of Omaha in partnership with Forefront Dermatology, 22nd group since 2018. - Chicago, Dec. 11, 2025.
data.description: Physician Growth Partners ("PGP") has successfully advised its 22nd dermatology group transaction since 2018 in Chicago. This highlights the firm's proficiency in facilitating successful mergers and acquisitions for dermatology and aesthetics practices.

data.title: Kuwait's automotive remanufacturing market is worth $1.2 billion, with growing demand for cost-effective and sustainable solutions.
data.description: A recent report on the Kuwait Automotive Remanufacturing market, available on ResearchAndMarkets.com, reveals that the market is currently valued at USD 1.2 billion. The report analyzes data from the past five years and forecasts trends up to 2030. The growth in this market is attributed to rising interest in affordable and environmentally-friendly automotive options. This includes remanufacturing engines, alternators, and turbos. The report provides insights on market size, share, growth drivers, trends, opportunities, and competitive landscape.

data.title: Mitsubishi HC Capital Canada predicts stable industrial outlook for 2026; highlights key trends shaping Canadian sector.
data.description: Mitsubishi HC Capital Canada predicts four key trends shaping Canada's industrial equipment sector in 2026. The sector is affected by cautious investment strategies, technology-driven transformation, and evolving trade dynamics due to global supply chain realignments and automation adoption. Manufacturers are investing in robotics and AI to address workforce gaps, while forklift/material handling companies are upgrading equipment for efficiency. Logistic firms are exploring warehouse automation and electrification to keep up with environmental regulations. Labor shortages are driving automation and upskilling, leading to a shift towards technology and efficiency in the industry. Mitsubishi advises companies to use flexible financing and innovative strategies for sustainable growth.

data.title: Manifest MedEx partners with NinePatch to launch social health information exchange in California, integrating SDOH info.
data.description: In Riverside, California and Colorado Springs, Colorado, Manifest MedEx (MX), the largest nonprofit health information organization in California, has partnered with NinePatch®, a top SaaS platform for Whole Person Health and Referral Information Exchange. They have announced a collaboration to introduce a statewide social health information exchange (SHIE) platform. This platform will integrate social determinants of health (SDOH) information and tools, including care assessments, program enrollment, closed-loop referrals, and consent. This initiative aims to combine vital social health data to enhance healthcare services.

data.title: Chewy Inc. beats Q3 earnings expectations with adjusted earnings of 32 cents/share and sales of $3.117 billion. CEO optimistic.
data.description: Chewy Inc. (NYSE: CHWY) exceeded expectations with their third-quarter 2025 earnings report. Adjusted earnings per share were reported at 32 cents, surpassing both the consensus estimate of 13 cents and falling within the predicted range of 28-33 cents as provided by management. The company, known for its pet supplies, showcased a revenue of $3.117 billion for the quarter, indicating an 8.3% increase compared to the previous year. This figure also outperformed the consensus of $3.099 billion and aligned closely with the management projection of $3.07 billion to $3.1 billion. CEO Sumit Singh stated that Chewy continues to excel in the pet industry, expanding market share while demonstrating profit growth that exceeds sales growth. Expectations for the fourth quarter's adjusted earnings are...

data.title: New Dickey’s Barbecue Pit opens in Littleton, Colorado with grand opening on December 13 featuring great meals, deals, and community support.
data.description: Littleton, Colorado has welcomed a new addition with the opening of Dickey’s Barbecue Pit. This latest location is looking to make a smoky impression, offering a variety of delicious meals to the community. The grand opening event on Saturday, December 13, promises a day filled with special deals and a chance to support a charitable cause. From 3:00 pm to 6:00 pm, there will be a live broadcast by Mike Boyle, adding to the festive atmosphere. Don't miss out on this opportunity to enjoy great food and give back to the community.

data.title: commercetools and Stripe partnership aids enterprises in AI-driven commerce, for faster sales via AI agents. BOSTON, Dec. 11, 2025 - PRNewswire.
data.description: A recent partnership has been established in Boston on December 11, 2025, aiming to streamline the process of discovering agents, facilitating checkouts, and managing payments for enterprises. This collaboration allows businesses to generate additional revenue through a unified integration. The use of AI in commerce is rapidly emerging as a significant avenue for growth, with millions of opportunities arising in this sector.

data.title: Roche's oral drug for breast cancer reduces recurrence by 30% in early-stage patients. Positive study results presented at 2025 Symposium.
data.description: Roche Holdings AG reported data from the phase 3 lidERA Breast Cancer study, which investigated giredestrant as adjuvant endocrine therapy for ER-positive, HER2-negative, early-stage breast cancer. The findings were presented at the 2025 San Antonio Breast Cancer Symposium. According to a press release on Wednesday, Roche stated that Giredestrant is the first oral SERD to exhibit superior iDFS in the adjuvant setting. LidERA marks the second positive phase 3 outcome for giredestrant, following the evERA Breast Cancer results. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

Ukraine has released video showing what it says are sea drones hitting a tanker they say is involved in trading Russian oil as it sailed through Ukraine's exclusive economic zone in the Black Sea to the Russian port of Novorossiysk.
